title: Israel Approves Purchase of F-35I Adir and F-15IA Fighters to Expand Air Superiority
summary: Israel greenlighted the purchase of approximately 50 advanced fighter jets from the US, including a full squadron of F-35I stealth fighters and F-15IA strike fighters.
contents:
## Israel Fighter Jet Acquisition — Defense Technology Analysis

### Procurement Details
Israel approved the purchase of approximately 50 advanced fighter jets from the United States, significantly expanding its air superiority capabilities. The deal includes one full squadron of F-35I Adir multi-role stealth fighters produced by Lockheed Martin and one squadron of F-15IA strike fighters from Boeing.

### Operational Context
Defense Minister Israel Katz stated: "Our mission demands ensuring the IDF possesses the tools, capabilities, and strength to operate anywhere, at any time." The F-35I Adir has served as one of the Israeli Air Force's most valuable assets during both the Gaza and Iran wars, particularly for suppressing air defenses and identifying deep targets.

### Technical Capabilities
The F-35I represents Israel's customized variant of the F-35 Lightning II, incorporating indigenous avionics and electronic warfare systems. The aircraft provides fifth-generation stealth capability, advanced sensor fusion, and multi-role mission flexibility. The F-15IA offers complementary long-range strike and air superiority capabilities with a larger weapons payload.

### Strategic Significance
The acquisition reflects lessons learned from ongoing combat operations against Iran and its proxies. Israel's extensive use of the F-35I in strikes against Iranian targets — including suppression of integrated air defense systems — has validated the platform's combat effectiveness and driven demand for expanded fleet capacity. The dual fighter purchase ensures both stealth penetration and heavy strike capabilities for sustained high-intensity operations.
